response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with over 15 years of experience, but she is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates native-level Japanese proficiency, which she entirely lacks—an outright disqualifier. Beyond the language barrier, her technical background is centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and web/cloud platforms rather than embedded C firmware, Linux kernel modification, or networking security protocols such as MACsec or IEEE 802.1X, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience spanning web, mobile, and some embedded systems work
Exposure to low-level C and C++ development through her role at National Instruments building IDE components
Strong Linux-based development background and a consistent track record of delivering production systems
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at all—this is a stated hard requirement and renders the candidate ineligible regardless of technical merit
Core skill set is TypeScript, Node.js, and web development, with minimal embedded C or firmware engineering experience
No demonstrated experience with networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Layer-2/3 switching, or Linux kernel-level development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security feature experience
General software engineering seniority and breadth
